structured.name: Authorization using Contract Certificates
structured.objective: See ISO15118-1, use case Objective D2, page 26.
structured.description: See ISO15118-1, use case Description D2 (first bullet), page 26.
scenario:
15118:
See ISO15118-1, use case Description D2, Scenario Description, first 2 bullets, page

3. The Charging Station sends an AuthorizeRequest message to the CSMS containing the eMAID
and data needed for an OCSP request with regards to the contract certificate and certificate
chain.
4. The CSMS replies with an agreement or non-agreement, and the certificate status.
5. Service starts after successful authorization of the IDs.
structured.prerequisites: A contract Certificate is installed in the EV.
structured.postconditions: The validity of the Contract Certificate is determined.
structured.remarks:
In edition 1 of 15118, the message timeout of the PaymentDetailsReq/Res message is 5 seconds.
In case certificate verification cannot be completed in that time it is possible to complete this
during the AuthorizationReq/Res, which can be extended up to 60 seconds.
When the Charging Station is offline, it is recommended to omit the payment option for ISO 15118
contract certificates from the ServiceDiscoveryRes and revert to External Identification Means
(use case C08), because certificate status cannot be checked.

requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL send an
AuthorizeRequest to the CSMS for validation.

The AuthorizeRequest SHALL contain the eMAID
and data needed for an OCSP request with regards
to the contract certificate and certificate chain.

requirements.precondition:
If the CSMS receives an
AuthorizeRequest.
requirements.definition:
It SHALL respond with an AuthorizeResponse and
SHALL include an authorization status value
indicating acceptance or a reason for rejection.

The CSMS SHALL verify validity of the certificate
and certificate chain via real-time or cached OCSP
data.

requirements.precondition:
C07.FR.01 AND
If Charging Station is not able to
validate a contract certificate,
because it does not have the
associated root certificate AND
CentralContractValidationAll
owed is true
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL pass the contract
certificate chain to the CSMS in certificate attribute
(in PEM format) of AuthorizeRequest for validation
by CSMS.

requirements.precondition:
When Charging Station is offline AND
ContractValidationOffline is
false
requirements.definition: The Charging Station SHALL NOT allow charging.

requirements.precondition:
When Charging Station is offline AND
ContractValidationOffline is
true
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL try to validate the
contract certificate locally.

requirements.precondition:
C07.FR.08 AND
Contract certificate is valid AND
LocalAuthorizeOffline is true
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL lookup the eMAID in
Local Authorization List or Authorization Cache.

requirements.precondition:
C07.FR.09 AND
eMAID found in Local Authorization
List
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL behave according to
use case C13 - Offline Authorization through Local
Authorization List.

requirements.precondition:
C07.FR.09 AND
eMAID found in Authorization Cache
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL behave according to
use case C12 - Start Transaction - Cached Id.

requirements.precondition:
C07.FR.09 AND
eMAID is not found AND
OfflineTxForUnknownIdEnabled
= true
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL allow charging
according to use case C15 - Offline Authorization of
unknown Id.

requirements.precondition:
C07.FR.04 AND
the certificate chain (provided in
certificate or
iso15118CertificateHashData) is valid
AND
authorization status of idToken is one
of Blocked, Expired, Invalid,
Unknown
requirements.definition:
CSMS SHALL return an AuthorizationResponse
containing a certificateStatus =
ContractCancelled and the authorization status
in idTokenInfo.status.

requirements.precondition:
C07.FR.04 AND
the certificate chain (provided in
certificate or
iso15118CertificateHashData) is valid
AND
authorization status of idToken is NOT
one of Blocked, Expired, Invalid,
Unknown
requirements.definition:
CSMS SHALL return an AuthorizationResponse
containing a certificateStatus = Accepted and the
authorization status in idTokenInfo.status.

requirements.precondition:
C07.FR.04 AND
the certificate chain (provided in
certificate or
iso15118CertificateHashData) has
expired
requirements.definition:
CSMS SHALL return an AuthorizationResponse
containing a certificateStatus =
CertificateExpired and an idTokenInfo.status
= Expired

requirements.precondition:
C07.FR.04 AND
the certificate chain (provided in
certificate or
iso15118CertificateHashData) has
been revoked
requirements.definition:
CSMS SHALL return an AuthorizationResponse
containing a certificateStatus =
CertificateRevoked and an idTokenInfo.status
= Invalid

requirements.precondition:
C07.FR.04 AND
the certificate chain (provided in
certificate or
iso15118CertificateHashData) cannot
be verified or is invalid
requirements.definition:
CSMS SHALL return an AuthorizationResponse
containing a certificateStatus = CertChainError
and an idTokenInfo.status = Invalid

chunks.content:
# C07 - Authorization using Contract Certificates

**Objective:** See ISO15118-1, use case Objective D2, page 26.

**Description:** See ISO15118-1, use case Description D2 (first bullet), page 26.

**Actors:** EV, Charging Station, CSMS

**Scenario:**
15118:
See ISO15118-1, use case Description D2, Scenario Description, first 2 bullets, page
6.
OCPP:
3. The Charging Station sends an AuthorizeRequest message to the CSMS containing the eMAID
and data needed for an OCSP request with regards to the contract certificate and certificate
chain.
4. The CSMS replies with an agreement or non-agreement, and the certificate status.
5. Service starts after successful authorization of the IDs.

**Prerequisites:** A contract Certificate is installed in the EV.

**Postconditions:** The validity of the Contract Certificate is determined.

**Remarks:** In edition 1 of 15118, the message timeout of the PaymentDetailsReq/Res message is 5 seconds.
In case certificate verification cannot be completed in that time it is possible to complete this
during the AuthorizationReq/Res, which can be extended up to 60 seconds.
When the Charging Station is offline, it is recommended to omit the payment option for ISO 15118
contract certificates from the ServiceDiscoveryRes and revert to External Identification Means
(use case C08), because certificate status cannot be checked.

# C07 - Requirements

**C07.FR.01**
Precondition: When Charging Station is online
The Charging Station SHALL send an
AuthorizeRequest to the CSMS for validation.

**C07.FR.02**
Precondition: C07.FR.01
The AuthorizeRequest SHALL contain the eMAID
and data needed for an OCSP request with regards
to the contract certificate and certificate chain.

**C07.FR.04**
Precondition: If the CSMS receives an
AuthorizeRequest.
It SHALL respond with an AuthorizeResponse and
SHALL include an authorization status value
indicating acceptance or a reason for rejection.

**C07.FR.05**
Precondition: C07.FR.02
The CSMS SHALL verify validity of the certificate
and certificate chain via real-time or cached OCSP
data.

**C07.FR.06**
Precondition: C07.FR.01 AND
If Charging Station is not able to
validate a contract certificate,
because it does not have the
associated root certificate AND
CentralContractValidationAll
owed is true
The Charging Station SHALL pass the contract
certificate chain to the CSMS in certificate attribute
(in PEM format) of AuthorizeRequest for validation
by CSMS.

**C07.FR.07**
Precondition: When Charging Station is offline AND
ContractValidationOffline is
false
The Charging Station SHALL NOT allow charging.

**C07.FR.08**
Precondition: When Charging Station is offline AND
ContractValidationOffline is
true
The Charging Station SHALL try to validate the
contract certificate locally.

**C07.FR.09**
Precondition: C07.FR.08 AND
Contract certificate is valid AND
LocalAuthorizeOffline is true
The Charging Station SHALL lookup the eMAID in
Local Authorization List or Authorization Cache.

**C07.FR.10**
Precondition: C07.FR.09 AND
eMAID found in Local Authorization
List
The Charging Station SHALL behave according to
use case C13 - Offline Authorization through Local
Authorization List.

**C07.FR.11**
Precondition: C07.FR.09 AND
eMAID found in Authorization Cache
The Charging Station SHALL behave according to
use case C12 - Start Transaction - Cached Id.

**C07.FR.12**
Precondition: C07.FR.09 AND
eMAID is not found AND
OfflineTxForUnknownIdEnabled
= true
The Charging Station SHALL allow charging
according to use case C15 - Offline Authorization of
unknown Id.

# C07 - Requirements (continued)

**C07.FR.13**
Precondition: C07.FR.04 AND
the certificate chain (provided in
certificate or
iso15118CertificateHashData) is valid
AND
authorization status of idToken is one
of Blocked, Expired, Invalid,
Unknown
CSMS SHALL return an AuthorizationResponse
containing a certificateStatus =
ContractCancelled and the authorization status
in idTokenInfo.status.

**C07.FR.14**
Precondition: C07.FR.04 AND
the certificate chain (provided in
certificate or
iso15118CertificateHashData) is valid
AND
authorization status of idToken is NOT
one of Blocked, Expired, Invalid,
Unknown
CSMS SHALL return an AuthorizationResponse
containing a certificateStatus = Accepted and the
authorization status in idTokenInfo.status.

**C07.FR.15**
Precondition: C07.FR.04 AND
the certificate chain (provided in
certificate or
iso15118CertificateHashData) has
expired
CSMS SHALL return an AuthorizationResponse
containing a certificateStatus =
CertificateExpired and an idTokenInfo.status
= Expired

**C07.FR.16**
Precondition: C07.FR.04 AND
the certificate chain (provided in
certificate or
iso15118CertificateHashData) has
been revoked
CSMS SHALL return an AuthorizationResponse
containing a certificateStatus =
CertificateRevoked and an idTokenInfo.status
= Invalid

**C07.FR.17**
Precondition: C07.FR.04 AND
the certificate chain (provided in
certificate or
iso15118CertificateHashData) cannot
be verified or is invalid
CSMS SHALL return an AuthorizationResponse
containing a certificateStatus = CertChainError
and an idTokenInfo.status = Invalid
